description:
You are a configuration manager who wants to begin tracking Python-package inventories for every project.  
Create the very first, minimal proof-of-concept on the system.

1. Make a **new** virtual environment inside the directory  
   /home/user/projects/cm_venv  
   (If the parent directory does not exist yet, you must create it first.)

2. After the environment is activated, capture its *initial* package list **before installing anything else**.  
   Produce that inventory with the `pip freeze` tool and save it to the file  

   /home/user/projects/config_track/env_packages.log

3. The log file must obey the following exact format so that the auditor’s automation can parse it later:  
   • One package per line.  
   • Each line is of the form `package_name==exact_version_number` (two consecutive equal signs).  
   • No blank lines, no leading-or-trailing spaces, and the lines must be in strict **alphabetical order**.  
   • The file must contain at least the packages that are present in a brand-new venv (for example, “pip” and “setuptools”).  
   • Do **not** add any commentary; the file must be pure package entries.

That’s all the auditor needs for this demo run.

Important notes for your shell session:

• Work only under /home/user (root privileges are not available).  
• Use `python3 -m venv` (or an equivalent standard tool already present) to build the environment; do not rely on virtualenv from PyPI.  
• Do not install or upgrade any packages—capture what the venv provides out of the box.  
• Make sure all directories you create are world-readable (the default `mkdir` permissions are fine).  

When you finish, the two directories and the single log file described above must exist exactly as specified, and the log file must follow the strict line-by-line format given.  The auditor’s checker will read that log to confirm success.
truth:
EXPECTED ARTIFACTS AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
------------------------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/projects/cm_venv
   • Must be a valid Python venv root; i.e. it contains the canonical sub-directories such as “bin”, “lib”, “pyvenv.cfg”.
   • The file /home/user/projects/cm_venv/pyvenv.cfg must exist and include a line beginning with “home =”.

2. Directory: /home/user/projects/config_track
   • Must exist and be readable.

3. File: /home/user/projects/config_track/env_packages.log
   • Must exist and be readable.
   • Must contain ONLY lines that match the regular expression “^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+==[0-9a-zA-Z.]+$”.
   • Lines must appear in pure ASCII sort order.
   • The checker will specifically verify that at least two lines start with:
        pip==
        setuptools==
   • The file must contain no duplicate lines and no extra whitespace.

The test harness will reject the submission if any other files are created in /home/user/projects/ or if the log violates the required format.
